title: Unique, sorted values
prompt_md:
Write `unique_sorted(items)` that returns a **sorted list** of the unique values in `items` (duplicates removed).

For example, `unique_sorted([3, 1, 2, 1, 3])` returns `[1, 2, 3]`. An empty list returns `[]`.

Return a **list** (not a set) so the order is predictable.
hints: set(items) removes duplicates, but a set has no order.
Wrap it in sorted(...) to get back an ordered list: sorted(set(items)).
